title: Hashcat Reference
html:
Hashcat Reference

GPU-accelerated password cracking. Essential for cracking AD hashes (NTLM, NetNTLMv2, Kerberos TGS/AS-REP) captured during assessments.

Common Hash Modes

# Mode  Hash Type
# ----  ---------
# 0     MD5
# 100   SHA1
# 400   phppass / WordPress
# 500   md5crypt (Unix)
# 1000  NTLM                          (Windows local/domain hashes)
# 1800  sha512crypt (Unix $6$)         (Linux /etc/shadow)
# 3000  LM                             (Legacy Windows)
# 5500  NetNTLMv1 / NTLMv1-ESS        (Responder capture)
# 5600  NetNTLMv2 / NTLMv2            (Responder capture — most common)
# 7300  IPMI2 RAKP HMAC-SHA1
# 13100 Kerberos TGS-REP RC4 ($krb5tgs$23$)   (Kerberoast)
# 18200 Kerberos AS-REP RC4 ($krb5asrep$23$)  (AS-REP Roast)
# 19600 Kerberos TGS-REP AES128 ($krb5tgs$17$)
# 19700 Kerberos TGS-REP AES256 ($krb5tgs$18$)
# 22100 BitLocker
# 13400 KeePass
# 22000 WPA-PBKDF2-PMKID+EAPOL

Basic Cracking

# Straight wordlist attack
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist>

# Common examples
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> <wordlist>          # NTLM
hashcat -m 5600 <hash-file> <wordlist>          # NetNTLMv2
hashcat -m 13100 <hash-file> <wordlist>         # Kerberoast RC4
hashcat -m 18200 <hash-file> <wordlist>         # AS-REP Roast

# Show already cracked hashes
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> --show
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> --show

Rules-Based Attacks

# Single rule file
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> -r <rules>

# Multiple rules (applied sequentially)
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/toggles1.rule

# Common rule files
hashcat -m 5600 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule
hashcat -m 5600 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/dive.rule
hashcat -m 5600 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/OneRuleToRuleThemAll.rule

# Generate rules on the fly
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> -g 1000

Mask Attacks (Brute Force Patterns)

# Attack mode 3 = mask/brute force
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> -a 3 <mask>

# Mask charset symbols:
# ?l = lowercase a-z
# ?u = uppercase A-Z
# ?d = digits 0-9
# ?s = special chars
# ?a = all printable (?l?u?d?s)

# Common corporate password patterns
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> -a 3 ?u?l?l?l?l?d?d?d?s   # Password1!
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> -a 3 ?u?l?l?l?l?l?d?d      # Welcome22
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> -a 3 ?u?l?l?l?l?l?l?d?d    # Password22

# 8-char all-charset brute
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> -a 3 ?a?a?a?a?a?a?a?a

# Incremental (try all lengths 1-8)
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> -a 3 --increment --increment-min 1 --increment-max 8 ?a?a?a?a?a?a?a?a

# Hybrid: wordlist + mask
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> -a 6 <wordlist> ?d?d?d?d
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> -a 7 ?d?d?d?d <wordlist>

AD Hash Quick Reference

# Kerberoast — RC4 (most common)
hashcat -m 13100 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 13100 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

# AS-REP Roast
hashcat -m 18200 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 18200 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

# NetNTLMv2 (Responder capture)
hashcat -m 5600 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 5600 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

# NTLM (from secretsdump)
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
hashcat -m 1000 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

# Full combo attack (wordlist + rules + mask)
hashcat -m 5600 <hash-file> /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/toggles1.rule

Useful Flags

# Optimize kernel (faster but limits password length)
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> -O

# Workload profile (1=low, 2=default, 3=high, 4=nightmare)
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> -w 3

# Force CPU cracking (no GPU)
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> --force

# Show session status
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> --status

# Save cracked passwords to file
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> -o /tmp/cracked.txt

# Output format (hashcat default: hash:plain)
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> --show --outfile-format 2   # plain only

# Resume interrupted session
hashcat --session mysession -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist>
hashcat --session mysession --restore

# Best combo for speed
hashcat -m <hash-mode> <hash-file> <wordlist> -O -w 3

John the Ripper Equivalents

# Basic wordlist
john <hash-file> --wordlist=<wordlist>

# Format specification
john <hash-file> --format=NT --wordlist=<wordlist>
john <hash-file> --format=krb5tgs --wordlist=<wordlist>       # Kerberoast
john <hash-file> --format=krb5asrep --wordlist=<wordlist>     # AS-REP
john <hash-file> --format=netntlmv2 --wordlist=<wordlist>     # NetNTLMv2

# With rules
john <hash-file> --wordlist=<wordlist> --rules
john <hash-file> --wordlist=<wordlist> --rules=jumbo

# Show cracked
john <hash-file> --show
john <hash-file> --show --format=NT

# Auto-detect format
john <hash-file> --wordlist=<wordlist> --format=auto

# Convert hashes for john (from /etc/shadow)
unshadow /etc/passwd /etc/shadow > /tmp/unshadowed.txt
john /tmp/unshadowed.txt --wordlist=<wordlist>
excerpt: Hashcat GPU password cracking reference. Hash modes: NTLM 1000, NetNTLMv2 5600, Kerberoast 13100, AS-REP 18200. Wordlist, rules, mask attacks, John the Ripper equivalents.
